intitule: Reading Comprehension: Keeping Informed about Weather Conditions

enonce.texte:
Read the weather report and answer the questions that follow.

WEEKLY WEATHER FORECAST

Monday: Sunny, high of 32°C, low of 22°C. Light winds from the east.

Tuesday: Partly cloudy, high of 30°C, low of 23°C. Chance of rain: 20%.

Wednesday: Thunderstorms, high of 28°C, low of 21°C. Heavy rain expected.

Thursday: Rain showers, high of 27°C, low of 20°C. Humidity: 80%.

Friday: Mostly sunny, high of 31°C, low of 22°C. Moderate winds from the west.

Saturday: Clear skies, high of 33°C, low of 23°C. Great weather for outdoor activities!

Sunday: Partly cloudy, high of 31°C, low of 22°C. Chance of rain: 30%.

Source: National Meteorological Service
images: A weather forecast infographic showing icons for sunny, partly cloudy, thunderstorms, and rain showers with temperature readings.

questions.texte: What is the weather forecast for Wednesday?
questions.reponse: Thunderstorms with heavy rain, high of 28°C and low of 21°C.

questions.texte: Which day has the highest temperature of the week?
List three days that have a chance of rain.
questions.reponse: Tuesday (20%), Wednesday (heavy rain), Thursday (rain showers), Sunday (30%) - any three.

questions.texte: Explain why Saturday would be a good day for outdoor activities.
questions.reponse: Saturday has clear skies, a high temperature of 33°C, and low temperature of 23°C, making it a pleasant day with good weather for outdoor activities.

questions.texte: Compare the weather conditions on Monday and Wednesday.
questions.reponse: Monday is sunny with a high of 32°C and low of 22°C, while Wednesday has thunderstorms with heavy rain, a high of 28°C, and low of 21°C. Monday is warmer and drier, while Wednesday is cooler and wetter.

enonce.texte: Combine the following pairs of sentences using relative clauses (who, which, that, whose, whom).

questions.texte: The meteorologist is very experienced. He gave the weather forecast.
questions.reponse: The meteorologist who gave the weather forecast is very experienced.

questions.texte: The storm caused a lot of damage. It hit the coastal areas.
questions.reponse: The storm which hit the coastal areas caused a lot of damage.

questions.texte: The woman is a climate scientist. Her research focuses on global warming.
questions.reponse: The woman whose research focuses on global warming is a climate scientist.

questions.texte: The city was heavily flooded. It received 100mm of rain.
questions.reponse: The city which received 100mm of rain was heavily flooded.

questions.texte: I met a researcher. He studies climate change.
questions.reponse: I met a researcher who studies climate change.

enonce.texte: Write a weather report for your city for the next three days. Include temperature, precipitation, and any warnings. Use at least three relative clauses in your report.

questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

Weather Forecast for Douala - November 15-17, 2025

The city of Douala, which is located in the coastal region of Cameroon, will experience warm and humid weather over the next three days. On Saturday, November 15, temperatures will reach a high of 34°C with a low of 25°C. The day will be partly cloudy, with a 30% chance of rain that is expected to fall in the afternoon. People who plan outdoor activities should be prepared for sudden showers.

Sunday, November 16, will be mostly sunny with a high of 35°C and a low of 26°C. The humidity, which often reaches 80%, will make it feel hotter than the actual temperature. It is advisable for those who are sensitive to heat to stay hydrated.

Monday, November 17, will bring thunderstorms that could cause flooding in low-lying areas. The heavy rain, which is expected in the morning, may disrupt traffic. Residents who live in flood-prone areas should take necessary precautions.

enonce.texte: Write a short poem (6-8 lines) about the weather. Use at least one relative clause.

questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

The sun that shines so bright and warm,
Brings joy and happiness to the farm.
The rain that falls from clouds above,
Brings life and growth for all we love.

The wind that whispers through the trees,
Brings cool relief and gentle breeze.
Each weather pattern has its role,
In nature's grand and perfect whole.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and underline the relative clause in each one.

questions.texte: The weather forecast which was on television predicted heavy rain.
People who live in coastal areas should prepare for storms.
The meteorologist whose advice we trust gave a warning.
The storm that hit the city caused a lot of damage.
The climate scientist whom I interviewed is very knowledgeable.

enonce.texte: Complete the dialogue between two friends discussing the weather. Use relative clauses where appropriate.

questions.texte:
A: Have you seen the weather forecast for today?

B: Yes, I saw it this morning. The forecast, _______ (1) was very accurate, predicts sunny weather.

A: That's great! I was planning to go to the beach. Do you think it's a good idea?

B: Actually, the weather report _______ (2) I heard says that the sunny weather will only last until noon. In the afternoon, there might be rain showers.

A: Oh, I see. My father, _______ (3) is a fisherman, said the sea looked calm this morning.

B: That's a good sign. People _______ (4) go fishing often know more about the weather than the forecasters!

A: I agree. But I think I'll go in the morning, _______ (5) is the best time for outdoor activities.
